page: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v5 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an ARMv8-based Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at 3.0 GHz, with a single thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 208.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.25 GB per core, alongside 2458 GB of local storage. The system operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and memory read performance. The instance achieves top-quartile results in multi-core stress-ng tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and software compilation with Clang. Memory read bandwidth is also in the top 25%, reaching 4383.55 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 176.92 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with weak single-core Geekbench and bzip3 decompression scores, alongside a lack of advanced vector extensions (AVX) and matrix extensions (AMX).

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture provides high physical core density and strong multi-threaded throughput relative to its resource profile. This makes the instance highly effective for database operations and key-value stores, as evidenced by strong Passmark database and Redis SET benchmarks. It is also suitable for multi-threaded software development, static web serving, and moderate language model inference, though it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or workloads requiring specialized x86 vector instructions.
description: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v5 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores on an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It is equipped with 208.0 GB of memory and 2458 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance delivers strong multi-core performance, software compilation speeds, and memory read bandwidth, as demonstrated by top-quartile benchmark results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Clang compilation. However, single-core performance remains a tradeoff, with lower scores in single-threaded benchmarks and a lack of AVX or AMX instruction support. Qualitatively, the ARM architecture offers high core density and resource efficiency for parallel workloads. It is well-suited for database operations, Redis deployments, multi-threaded build systems, and static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v5 is an ARM-based server featuring 64 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 208 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v5 is an ARM-based server with 64 dedicated cores, 208 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered server offering sixty-four dedicated cores and local storage for highly parallel workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv8 Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores running at 3.0 GHz
208.0 GB of system memory and 2458 GB of local storage
Strong multi-core performance and high memory read bandwidth
Tradeoffs include lower single-core speeds and lack of AVX/AMX support
Suited for database operations, Redis, and multi-threaded software compilation
